output: {"domain":"veterinary_diagnostics","task":"Step‑by‑step diagnostic and management plan for a 12‑year‑old female Golden Retriever with an abdominal mass suspected to be splenic hemangiosarcoma.","solution_steps":[{"step":1,"description":"Confirm clinical picture – review history, note age, breed, weight, presenting signs of moderate progressive abdominal distension and mild lethargy."},{"step":2,"description":"Interpret current laboratory data – mild anemia suggests chronic disease or occult bleeding; mildly elevated ALP may indicate hepatic involvement or bone turnover."},{"step":3,"description":"Summarize imaging findings – ultrasound shows a large splenic mass consistent with hemangiosarcoma; thoracic radiographs negative for metastasis."},{"step":4,"description":"Assess cytology result – FNA yielded only blood; recommend repeat FNA or core needle biopsy for histopathology and immunohistochemistry."},{"step":5,"description":"Generate differential diagnoses – primary: splenic hemangiosarcoma, lymphoma, metastatic carcinoma, benign splenic tumors; secondary: abscess or granuloma."},{"step":6,"description":"Plan additional diagnostics – coagulation profile (PT/INR), baseline cardiac evaluation, abdominal CT/MRI for staging, core needle biopsy with CD31/vWF staining."},{"step":7,"description":"Discuss treatment options – surgical splenectomy as gold standard; adjuvant chemotherapy (doxorubicin or carboplatin); palliative care if surgery contraindicated."},{"step":8,"description":"Owner communication plan – explain likely diagnosis, prognosis, risks, costs, and decision‑making framework."},{"step":9,"description":"Follow‑up schedule – post‑operative monitoring, chemotherapy toxicity checks, regular imaging every 3–6 months."},{"step":10,"description":"Documentation – record findings, consent, treatment plan, and follow‑up in the medical record."}],"quality_self_assessment":{"score":0.95,"justification":"Evidence-based guidelines for splenic hemangiosarcoma were applied; comprehensive steps cover diagnostics, treatment, owner communication, and follow‑up."},"brain_assisted":false,"recalled_skill":"none","confidence":0.95}
